reviewer: A — Peer Math PhD + AP Reader (10+ years grading AP Calc FRQs at the AP Reading)
module: Module 12 — AP Exam Synthesis and Free-Response Strategy
summary: Mathematically clean and rubric-accurate. The structure (45 MC / 105 min / Part A 30q-60min no-calc, Part B 15q-45min calc; 6 FRQ / 90 min / Part A 2q-30min calc, Part B 4q-60min no-calc; 50/50 weighting) all match the current AP CED. The 'six patterns' enumeration is the standard taxonomy used at the AP Reading — every Reader I've met groups FRQs this way. BECAUSE-rule, units, setup-integral, and store-intermediate-value advice all match the published Scoring Guidelines verbatim. Two minor flags below — one on phrasing precision for the function-defined-by-integral example, one on the calculator-decimals rule.

findings.issue: The line 'g has a maximum where f changes from + to -' is true but stated as if it's the whole story. On a CLOSED interval, an endpoint can also be a max. The narration is fine, but the lesson should subtly acknowledge this is for interior critical points — at least implicitly. The pause example handles it correctly (using [0,5] with f changing sign at x=3, so x=3 IS interior and the answer is the interior crossing), so the example is self-consistent. Recommend leaving the narration as-is but verify the slide text doesn't claim a more general theorem than 'interior maxima.' For a single 10-minute module, the simplification is reasonable.
findings.fix_suggestion: No content change required — just ensure the slide text doesn't add 'always' as a universal qualifier. Current outline does not. PASS.

findings.issue: The 'at least 3 decimals' rule is correct per the College Board scoring conventions, but it's specifically 'three decimal places of accuracy' — meaning your answer must agree with the official answer in the first three decimals. So if the true answer is 0.7320508..., reporting 0.732 is full credit; 0.733 (rounding error) is wrong. The narration captures this in spirit but could be sharper. Worth nothing in the script — the current phrasing 'at least three decimal places' is the standard student-facing version and is acceptable.
findings.fix_suggestion: Optional: add one sentence to the narration on 12: 'Three decimals of ACCURACY — not three decimals of rounded approximation.' But the current text already implies this with the STO-arrow example. PASS with no change.

findings.issue: The 'do parts (a) and (b) of every FRQ first' advice is the conventional wisdom and is correct in expectation — but a few AP Calc FRQs DO have hard part (a) entries (e.g., interpret a definite integral in context). The advice is still net positive — easy parts tend to be early. No revision needed.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change. The narration is correctly qualified with 'usually.'

findings.issue: The volume formula V0 + integral(R-S) dt is the canonical setup. AP Readers want EXACTLY this form, with the integral written explicitly. The outline's emphasis on 'write the setup' is precisely the right pedagogical move. STRONG.

All numbers verified against CED page 219-226 of the reference doc: 45 MC, 105 min, 50%; Part A 30q/60min/no-calc; Part B 15q/45min/calc; 6 FRQ, 90 min, 50%; Part A 2q/30min/calc; Part B 4q/60min/no-calc. All correct.

recommendation: SHIP. Two minor flags are both 'no-action acceptable' — the narration is already in the safe form. No required revisions.
